items.title: S&P 500 closes higher as chip stocks rise and oil prices slide
items.content: US500 rose to 7575 points on July 10, gaining 0.42%. Dow closed at 52,487.41 (+0.27%), Nasdaq Composite +1.30% to 26,206.89, S&P 500 +0.81% to 7,543.64. Markets up 2.45% over the past month and 21.02% year-over-year.

items.title: Dow surpasses 53K; sets new closing record after holiday weekend
items.content: Dow Jones Industrial Average finished at a record high after the July 4 holiday weekend, surpassing 53,000 for the first time before closing at 52,925.15.

items.title: Hong Kong stocks rise as rallies from Alibaba, SMIC lift market sentiment
items.content: Hang Seng Index surged 5.8% to close at 24,208.83 on July 8, gaining 1,327.81 points. The Hang Seng Tech Index heavily outpaced the broader market. Lock-up expiry concerns eased after being absorbed without triggering sell-off.

items.title: Hang Seng falls 0.7% to 24,030 as tech volatility overshadows busy IPO pipeline
items.content: Hang Seng fell 169 points to close at 24,030.18, reversing part of Wednesday's sharp 3.0% rally. Notable gainers included Xiaomi (3.4%), Lenovo (1.8%), HKEX (6.2%), Meituan (0.3%), and Kuaishou (2.2%).

items.title: May US PCE inflation tops 4%, leaves Fed hike on the table
items.content: US PCE price index rose 4.1% annual rate in May, the highest since April 2023. Core PCE (ex-food/energy) at 3.4% year-over-year. Higher energy costs linked to Iran conflict continuing to push prices up.

items.title: US PCE price index expected to rise 0.5% MoM in May 2026
items.content: PCE price index rose 0.5% MoM in May, accelerating from 0.4% in April. Annual PCE increased to 4.10% from 3.80%. Fed's June meeting maintained rates but left door open for hikes.

items.title: US and Iran threaten return to war after fiercest exchange of fire since truce
items.content: US and Iran engaged in their fiercest exchange of fire since the April ceasefire. Tehran insists ships use a northern passage through Strait of Hormuz; US-sponsored southern route near Omani coast disputed. Trump said at NATO summit he thought the ceasefire was over.

items.title: 2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis continues as Iran re-closes strait
items.content: On June 19, Trump announced renewed ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah. Iran re-closed Strait of Hormuz on June 20 citing Israeli violations. US Navy announced widened route through Strait on June 27. Ceasefire status remains fragile.

items.title: China, Hong Kong stocks gain as chip, AI rally lead rebound
items.content: CSI Semiconductor Index rallied 4.1% and CSI AI Index jumped 3.9%. Mainland chip stocks surged on domestic substitution momentum. SMIC and ChangXin Memory Technologies leading semiconductor rally.

items.title: China Chip and AI Stocks offset inflation-led sector splits
items.content: Mainland semiconductor shares climbed 3.1% and STAR 50 rose 3.2%. Changxin Memory Technologies (CXMT) planning Shanghai IPO targeting 29.5 billion yuan ($4.34 billion), book-building starting July 15.

items.title: AI chip stocks - year's hottest rally losing steam
items.content: AI chip stocks that fueled Wall Street's powerful rally are under pressure as investors evaluate rising risks. Market questioning sustainability of AI-driven gains amid deteriorating macro environment.

items.title: Earnings Calendar for July 13-17: Netflix in focus
items.content: Q2 earnings season begins in earnest. Netflix faces key questions on engagement trends and 2026 margin guidance. Multiple reports scheduled for the week with heavy focus on tech sector results.
